The '''''Eleemosynary''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] that served as the personal [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] of the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Osvald Teshik]]. The [[Warship/Legends|warship]] was one of many [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyers]] that comprised the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], while Teshik was aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. The Grand Admiral narrowly escaped the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] destruction and retreated to the ''Eleemosynary'', from where he continued to combat the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] for three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] after the Imperial withdrawal was initiated. However, the Alliance eventually disabled the ''Eleemosynary'' with concentrated [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire and captured Teshik.

==Description==
The ''Eleemosynary'' was a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]]. [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Osvald Teshik]] used the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] as his personal [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

==History==
By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the ''Eleemosynary'' was in use as the command [[Starship/Legends|ship]] of the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] Grand Admiral Osvald Teshik. The Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyer]] was present in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] while Teshik was aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]],<ref name="Who's Who" /> a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] undergoing construction above the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. Along with the bulk of the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]], also stationed in the [[Star system/Legends|system]],<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> the ''Eleemosynary'' participated in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Navy]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

As the battle raged on, the Alliance succeeded in destroying the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]].<ref name="ROTJ" /> Teshik was able to escape the battlestation before its destruction, returning to the ''Eleemosynary''. When an Imperial retreat was sounded, Teshik opted to remain behind in the Endor system with his command ship and continue to fight the Rebels. Three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, the Alliance succeeded in disabling the ''Eleemosynary'' with a barrage of [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire.<ref name="Who's Who" /> The destroyer and its [[Commander/Legends|commander]] were both captured by Alliance forces;<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|233|Teshik, Grand Admiral Osvald}}</ref> Teshik was later [[Death/Legends|executed]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

==Commanders and crew==
The Star Destroyer ''Eleemosynary'' was commanded by Osvald Teshik, a Grand Admiral whose body was seventy-five percent [[Cybernetics/Legends|artificial]]. Teshik was a tactical genius dedicated to serving the Empire, standing his ground even when faced with overwhelming odds against success. This was evident in the closing stage of the Battle of Endor, but his refusal to retreat resulted in his capture by the Alliance and his eventual execution by the Alliance's successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|'''NOTE:''' '''''Eleemosynary''''' means '''''supported by charity.''''' (I had to look that up in the dictionary and have no idea how it relates to Teshik, so that must have been one of Abel's.)|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|danwallace|16|Grand Admirals: What Have They Got in their Pocketsses?}}</ref>}}
The ''Eleemosynary'' was first mentioned in "[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]," an article by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] that appeared in [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]].<ref name="Who's Who" /> In a blog entry on [[StarWars.com]], Wallace explained that "eleemosynary" is defined as "supported by charity" and that Peña likely applied the name to Teshik's command ship.<ref name="Blog" />

In an [[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals|early version]] of "Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" written by Wallace and Peña for ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine, the ''Eleemosynary'' was established to be an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="Blog" /> When the article was reworked for inclusion in ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', several details regarding the Grand Admirals and their [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] were omitted. The ''Eleemosynary''{{'s}} class was one such piece of information that was cut.<ref name="Who's Who" />

While "Who's Who" indicates that the Imperial retreat was sounded three hours before Teshik and his ship were captured by the Alliance,<ref name="Who's Who" /> ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] of the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] ordered the withdrawal after the ''Eleemosynary'' was disabled, four hours after the second Death Star's destruction.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> This article assumes the chronology presented in "Who's Who" is correct.
